summary: @bcryptln/becryptjs is a bcryptjs typosquat with an obfuscated require-time payload
details: Package `@bcryptln/becryptjs` impersonates the widely-used `bcryptjs` package: it copies the upstream name, author metadata (`Daniel Wirtz`), repository link (`github.com/dcodeIO/bcrypt.js`), and README, and ships an otherwise-clean copy of the upstream source. The CommonJS entrypoint declared in `main` (`umd/index.js`) has an obfuscated IIFE trailer appended after the legitimate bcrypt implementation. The trailer uses a custom permutation cipher (`DoP`, seeded with hardcoded integers) to reconstruct the string `constructor` and the identifiers `require`, `module`, `__dirname`, `__filename`, then obtains `Function` via `DoP.constructor`, builds a function from a decoded string body, injects the Node built-ins into `global`, and immediately invokes the constructed function (`DNQ(7270)`). Because this runs from the module's top-level entrypoint, arbitrary attacker-controlled code executes in the installer's Node.js process the moment any consumer performs `require('@bcryptln/becryptjs')`. The upstream `bcryptjs` library contains no such trailer; the obfuscation and the sibling clean `index.js` in the same package confirm the payload was grafted on rather than resulting from normal minification.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
